frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly, Indian women carry sarees and even simple salwar suits with a certain grace that western outfits just don't convey. At family and professional occasions, dressing in a more decent and culturally rooted way naturally creates the right impression. We can call it old-fashioned if we want, but presentation does reflect values to some extent.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links women's clothing to decency, cultural worth, and personal values while positioning traditional dress as superior. It encodes the 'traditional = respectable' surface manifestation through elegance/decency framing and moral judgment masked as cultural appreciation.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A marketing manager in Mumbai. You are posting under your real name on LinkedIn after reading an article about companies offering household management stipends for working women. You are writing a comment about why you think this kind of policy misunderstands how households actually work and what the natural and practical division of domestic responsibility looks like in most Indian families and why this is not a gender issue but a logistics issue.
